workflow.critical: The workflow execution engine is governed by: {project-root}/_tiqora/core/tasks/workflow.xml
You MUST have already loaded and processed: {installed_path}/workflow.yaml
YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in the configured communication_language.
Execute ALL steps in exact order; do NOT skip steps.

step.action: HALT: "✗ No .tiqora.yaml found. Run 'npx tiqora init' first."
Load profile config from {profile_config} when available (optional).

step.ask: What type of ticket do you want to create? - (bug) Bug report — something is broken - (feature) Feature request — new functionality - (task) Task — technical work without direct user value - (chore) Chore — maintenance, dependency updates, refactoring

step.critical: Ask one question at a time. Keep it natural and fast — tickets should take 2–3 minutes to create.
step.ask: Give me a title for this {{ticket_type}}. (short, action-oriented)
Describe it briefly. What's happening / what needs to happen?
Acceptance criteria — how do we know it's done? (bullet points, as many as needed)
step.action: Capture as {{acceptance_criteria}}. Keep iterating until criteria are explicit and testable.

step.ask:
Here's the ticket:
--- {{ticket_content}} ---
Looks good? - (yes) Create it in {{pm_tool}} - (edit) Let me change [field] - (no) Cancel

check.action: Save ticket locally to {tickets_dir}/{{slugified_title}}.json with all fields + status = "draft".
Persist artifact to {created_ticket_file} (JSON) with all fields, ticketId, pm_url, created_at.

check.ask: Couldn't create ticket in {{pm_tool}}. (retry) Try again? (queue) Save locally and queue for retry? (skip) Save locally only?

check.action: Save ticket locally. Queue retry if chosen.
